testcase.name: upgraded repository keeps legacy catalog Stories operable while the selected Story uses a named profile
unconfigured repository preserves current artifact defaults
named artifact-routing profile: schema 0.2 selects a complete profile from catalog authority
named profile requires a complete matching Story mirror before writes
catalog metadata authority and Story frontmatter conflict fail closed
task JSON remains read authority after Markdown regeneration
artifact resolve text and JSON expose ownership, canonical authority, profile, and renderer
projection lineage hashes exact routed canonical bytes and migration classifies noop then update
schema 0.1 generated projections retain legacy byte-copy overwrite compatibility
migration classifies stale generated lineage metadata as update, not conflict
named profiles reject unknown renderer contracts before writing
schema 0.2 validates every profile is complete, including an unselected profile

testsuite.name: schema 0.2 requires a non-empty canonical for every kind in selected and unselected profiles

testcase.name: feature_packet
governance_packet
schema 0.2 rejects repository-global legacy artifacts instead of silently falling back
projectArtifact preflights generated projections before canonical mutation
directory canonical migration hashes the lineage source file
directory projection may advance lineage to another file in the same canonical directory only
directory migration refuses a lineage source symlink that escapes the repository
Functional Spec renderer preserves actual statement origin diagram shape in code-point order
human-owned and curated bytes are never overwritten and schema 0.2 never falls back to legacy metadata
review gate and release views expose ownership and lineage to users
custom nested feature routing expands stable variables
tracked routing is restored and resolved in a fresh checkout
fresh checkout resolves and renders complete feature and governance packet lifecycle surfaces
unconfigured fresh checkout preserves legacy artifact defaults
artifact routing guides document configuration, migration, compatibility, and rollback

testsuite.name: routing fails closed for collisions, traversal, absolute paths, and unresolved variables

testcase.name: path_collision
repository_traversal
absolute_path
unresolved_variable
malformed routing config fails closed before changing repository files
projections must be explicitly machine generated
migration dry-run reports moves and collisions without editing files
migration dry-run blocks when source and destination contain different content
task plan migration treats a manifest-bound generated legacy mirror as noop but unknown Markdown as conflict
migration dry-run reports invalid routing as a blocked machine-readable plan
write containment rejects a configured path through a symlink outside the repository
architecture and accepted spec read and write the same configured canonical paths
generated projections mirror canonical writes but are never selected as read authority
unsafe projection fails before changing the canonical artifact
accepted spec projection failure leaves canonical and history untouched
story task projection failure creates neither task state nor task plan
plan task projection failure leaves tasks and manifest routing fields untouched
task plan writes its human-readable canonical to the configured feature packet
task plan JSON writers honor a custom routed canonical instead of the legacy workspace path
projections fail closed for artifact kinds without a centralized writer
directory artifact routes cannot contain another artifact destination
file artifact routes cannot be ancestors of directory artifact routes
Story Architecture Spec Task Graphify Review Gate PR status migration use one profile
named profile Graphify review and PR producers write only routed lifecycle paths
story derive binds global Graphify import to the configured current Story under schema 0.2
design-system derive binds Graphify evidence with --story-id

story-vibepro-routing-profiles-rendered-projections ac:1 ac:2 ac:3 ac:4 ac:5 ac:6 ac:7 ac:8 ac:9 ac:10 ac:11 ac:12 ac:13 ac:14 scenario:1 scenario:2 scenario:3 scenario:4 replays the routed artifact lifecycle
